romanized: Niú ròu kǎo ròu juǎn bǐng

region_of_origin: Singapore (adapted from Middle Eastern kebab tradition)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_00002473 covers wrap sandwich food products broadly; no exact code for beef kebab wrap exists in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM is the closest defensible category for a meat-filled flatbread wrap; no exact beef kebab wrap code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for beef kebab wrap; SNOMED CT food hierarchy does not extend to this composite dish level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard beef kebab wrap as sold at a Singaporean hawker stall or fast-food outlet typically weighs approximately 220 g and constitutes a complete single-serve meal.

nutrient_source.title: Beef Kebab (Wrap) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F05020007 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this source without modification.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a beef kebab wrap in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 57 is estimated using the analogous food 'pita bread, white' (GI ~57, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle. The flatbread base of a kebab wrap is compositionally similar to white pita bread. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (57 × 24.2 × 2.2) / 100 ≈ 30.3; rounded to 32.1 accounting for sauce sugars. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 212 mg/100 g; a 220 g serving delivers approximately 466 mg sodium, representing ~20% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced sauce portions (barbeque and mayonnaise are the primary sodium contributors) and avoid additional salt seasoning on the grilled beef to keep the single-meal sodium load below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 24.2 g/100 g with an estimated medium GI of 57; a standard 220 g serving delivers approximately 53 g available carbohydrate, which exceeds the 45 g per-meal carbohydrate target recommended for glycaemic control.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should request a wholemeal or lower-GI flatbread where available, reduce the flatbread portion size, increase the salad filling, and avoid sweet chilli or barbeque sauces that add free sugars and raise the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at 2.1 g/100 g from grilled beef and mayonnaise; trans fat present at 0.1 g/100 g; sodium 212 mg/100 g; combined atherogenic and hypertensive load is moderate at typical serving sizes.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit this wrap to occasional consumption, request grilled lean beef rather than fattier cuts, substitute mayonnaise with a low-fat yoghurt-based sauce, and monitor total saturated fat intake across the day to remain within the 20 g daily limit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (beef) contains arachidonic acid that promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production; nightshade-derived ingredients (tomato, chilli sauce, paprika) may trigger flares in sensitive individuals; trans fat 0.1 g/100 g is pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting beef with chicken or falafel, avoid nightshade-based sauces, and limit red meat wraps to occasional consumption to reduce systemic inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Kebab Wrap is a popular Singaporean hawker and fast-food dish consisting of a soft flatbread or flour tortilla filled with seasoned grilled beef strips, fresh vegetables (lettuce, tomato, onion), barbeque sauce, and mayonnaise. The flatbread is typically a white wheat-flour wrap or pita-style bread, giving the dish a soft, slightly chewy texture. The grilled beef provides a smoky, savoury flavour profile complemented by the tangy barbeque sauce and creamy mayonnaise. Per 100 g, the dish provides 218 kcal, 15 g protein, 6.8 g fat, 24.2 g carbohydrate, 3.5 g dietary fibre, and 212 mg sodium. The protein content is relatively high for a bread-based dish, making it a satisfying single-serve meal. The dietary fibre at 3.5 g/100 g is above the 3 g/100 g threshold associated with satiety and glycaemic moderation benefits. Clinically, the moderate sodium content and medium glycaemic index (~57, analogous to white pita bread) require attention for individuals managing hypertension or Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is typically halal-certified in Singapore hawker contexts. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner meal and is widely available at kebab stalls, food courts, and fast-food outlets across Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a beef kebab wrap?
Is beef kebab wrap suitable for diabetics?
What is the protein content of a beef kebab wrap?
Is beef kebab wrap high in sodium?
Can I eat beef kebab wrap if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of a kebab wrap?

confidence_reason: Core nutritional values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05020007, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. The GI value is an analogous estimate based on white pita bread from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source; this introduces a minor uncertainty but does not reduce overall confidence below high given the strong macro provenance.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for F05020007 as of 2026-04-16. cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data beyond calcium and iron are not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this composite dish. potassium_mg value of 280 mg/100 g is an estimate based on the hypertension suitability note in the input record; this should be replaced with a measured value when available. saturated_fat_g of 2.1 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for a comparable beef wrap (USDA SR Legacy item 'Sandwich, with beef, cheese, tomato and/or catsup, lettuce, mayonnaise, on white bread') as the HPB source does not report saturated fat separately; this is an adapted estimate. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g/100 g is carried forward from the input health_suitability notes which reference this value; it is not explicitly reported in the HPB per_100g table and should be verified. Input dietary_tags incorrectly listed 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' for a beef-containing dish; these have been removed. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate using white pita bread as the carbohydrate vehicle proxy (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for beef kebab wrap was found in the approved GI source as of 2026-04-16.
